pi_items.source: Downloaded and analyzed t1-head.tif using Python PIL: 256x256 XY dimensions with 129 Z frames
Task description specifies Max Intensity projection via Image > Stacks > Z Project
Logical consequence of projecting a 256x256x129 stack; single XY slice output

pi_items.metadata_value: Fiji built-in sample (T1 Head CT scan)
pi_items.source: https://imagej.net/ij/images/t1-head.txt explicitly states 'T1 weighted MRI head dataset courtesy of Jeff Orchard'; https://cs.uwaterloo.ca/~jorchard/ confirms this is MRI data

PIL analysis confirms 16-bit unsigned integer grayscale (Mode: I;16B); t1-head.txt documentation confirms 'T1 weighted MRI head dataset', not CT scan. T1-weighted is MRI terminology with no equivalent in CT scanning.

privileged_info_summary: The T1 Head sample is a real 256×256×129 voxel, 16-bit unsigned integer grayscale image available as a built-in sample in Fiji/ImageJ. Critically, the image is a T1-weighted MRI scan of a human head from Jeff Orchard's dataset, NOT a CT scan as claimed in the task metadata. The terminology 'T1-weighted' is specific to MRI imaging and refers to T1 relaxation time weighting. The file is located in the ImageJ sample images repository at imagej.net/ij/images/.
